root_cause_hypothesis: POSIX byte-range lock metadata in LockMeta does not implement Linux F_GETLK/F_SETLK semantics: partial unlock removes whole locks instead of splitting, same-owner overlapping sets accumulate stale ranges, F_GETLK returns an arbitrary conflicting lock instead of the earliest overlap, flush unconditionally releases POSIX locks on every FUSE_FLUSH leaving stale/conflicting state, and get_lk masks holder pid when client_id differs.
planned_changes: Implement inclusive region overlap/split helpers and rewrite POSIX lock set/check paths for partial unlock, same-owner replacement, and earliest-conflict F_GETLK reporting.
Only release POSIX locks on FUSE_FLUSH when lock_owner is non-zero, targeting that owner instead of unconditionally clearing handle plock state.
Return conflicting lock holder pid from get_lk regardless of client_id match.
not_goals: Do not implement kernel-style deadlock detection (EDEADLK) for fcntl17 in this change.
Do not change BSD flock semantics beyond shared cleanup paths.
Do not modify unrelated LTP failure groups (mknod, fallocate, FUSE EIO).
verification_plan: cargo test -p curvine-tests --test fs_test get_lock set_lock

risk.rationale: Touches distributed lock metadata semantics used by FUSE and RPC clients; incorrect region math could regress existing fs_test lock coverage or introduce new lock leaks.
